revision.comment: 「従六位」から別項化しました
revision.text:
'''正九位'''（しょうくい）は、[[日本]]の[[位階]]における位の一つ。[[従八位]]の下、[[従九位]]の上の位階である。
正九位は、[[明治時代]]初期の[[太政官制]]において、[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）7月に制定され同年[[8月22日 (旧暦)|8月22日]]<ref name=enkaku>内閣記録局『単行書・明治職官沿革表・職官部・一』（国立公文書館（ref.A07090183000））では、[[7月8日 (旧暦)|7月8日]]制定、[[8月20日 (旧暦)|8月20日]]改正とされている。</ref>に定められた職員令により、設けられた<ref name=soutou>[http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/BIImgFrame.php?JP_NUM=40022968&VOL_NUM=00004&KOMA=168&ITYPE=0 法令全書「明治2年」]、国立国会図書館。</ref>。正九位は、[[神祇官]]の官掌、[[民部省]]の史生などに相当する。
栄典としての位階制が定められた叙位条例（[[明治]]20年勅令第10号）、位階令（[[大正]]15年勅令第325号）には、九位はない。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://law.e-gov.go.jp/htmldata/T15/T15CO325.html 位階令] {{ja icon}} - 法令データ提供システム * [http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/BIImgFrame.php?JP_NUM=40022968&VOL_NUM=00004&KOMA=168&ITYPE=0 官位相当表] {{ja icon}} - 国立国会図書館近代デジタルライブラリー
{{日本の位階}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しようくい}} [[Category:日本の位階|しようくい]]

revision.text:
[[Category:Completion tier 2]] '''Shōkyū-i''' (正九位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank}} in the hierarchy.
== Overview ==
Shōkyū-i was established in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}. It was defined by the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令}} issued in {{ill|July 1869|ja|1869年7月}} and amended on {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869年8月22日}}.<ref group="notes" name="enkaku">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunar July8日}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunar August20日}}.</ref><ref name=soutou>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2年」], National Diet Library.</ref> It corresponded to positions such as officials of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank= (明治時代)}} and scribes of the {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|ja|民部省 (明治時代)}}.
In the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no rank corresponding to 九位 (Kyū-i) was included.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}}

[[Category:Completion tier 3]] '''Shōkyū-i''' (正九位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank}} in the hierarchy.
== Overview ==
Shōkyū-i was established in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}. It was defined by the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令}} issued in {{ill|July 1869|ja|1869年7月}} and amended on {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869年8月22日}}.<ref group="notes" name="enkaku">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunar July8日}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunar August20日}}.</ref><ref name=soutou>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2年」], National Diet Library.</ref> It corresponded to positions such as officials of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank= (明治時代)}} and scribes of the {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|ja|民部省 (明治時代)}}.
In the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no rank corresponding to 九位 (Kyū-i) was included.

revision.text:
[[Category:Court Ranks of Japan]][[Category:completion tier 4]] '''Shōkyū-i''' (正九位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank}} in the hierarchy.
== Overview ==
Shōkyū-i was established in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}. It was defined by the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令}} issued in {{ill|July 1869|ja|1869年7月}} and amended on {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869年8月22日}}.<ref group="notes" name="enkaku">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunar July8日}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunar August20日}}.</ref><ref name=soutou>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2年」], National Diet Library.</ref> It corresponded to positions such as officials of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank= (明治時代)}} and scribes of the {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|ja|民部省 (明治時代)}}.
In the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no rank corresponding to 九位 (Kyū-i) was included.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "2年" to "2nd year "
revision.text:
[[Category:Court Ranks of Japan]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] '''Shōkyū-i''' (正九位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-ranks/court-ranks/</ref>
== Overview ==
Shōkyū-i was established in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}. It was defined by the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令}} issued in {{ill|July 1869|ja|1869年7月}} and amended on {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869年8月22日}}.<ref group="notes" name="enkaku">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunar July8日}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunar August20日}}.</ref><ref name=soutou>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref> It corresponded to positions such as officials of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank= (明治時代)}} and scribes of the {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|ja|民部省 (明治時代)}}.
In the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no rank corresponding to 九位 (Kyū-i) was included.

[[Category:Court Ranks of Japan]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] '''Shōkyū-i''' (正九位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-ranks/court-ranks/</ref>
== Overview ==
Shōkyū-i was established in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}. It was defined by the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令}} issued in {{ill|July 1869|ja|1869th year 7月}} and amended on {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日}}.<ref group="notes" name="enkaku">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日}}.</ref><ref name=soutou>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref> It corresponded to positions such as officials of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank= (明治時代)}} and scribes of the {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|ja|民部省 (明治時代)}}.
In the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no rank corresponding to 九位 (Kyū-i) was included.

revision.text:
[[Category:Court Ranks of Japan]][[Category:completion tier 4]]'''Shōkyū-i''' (正九位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}} and above {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-ranks/court-ranks/</ref>
== Overview ==
Shōkyū-i was established in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制}}. It was defined by the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令}} issued in {{ill|July 1869|ja|1869th year 7月}} and amended on {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日}}.<ref group="notes" name="enkaku">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日}}.</ref><ref name=soutou>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref> It corresponded to positions such as officials of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank= (明治時代)}} and scribes of the {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|ja|民部省 (明治時代)}}.
In the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no rank corresponding to 九位 (Kyū-i) was included.

'''Shōkyū-i''' (正九位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階|12=draft}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=draft}} and above {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|12=draft}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-ranks/court-ranks/</ref>
== Overview ==
Shōkyū-i was established in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=draft}}. It was defined by the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=draft}} issued in {{ill|July 1869|ja|1869th year 7月|12=draft}} and amended on {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=draft}}.<ref group="notes" name="enkaku">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=draft}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=draft}}.</ref><ref name=soutou>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref> It corresponded to positions such as officials of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank= (明治時代)|12=draft}} and scribes of the {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|ja|民部省 (明治時代)|12=draft}}.
In the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=draft}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=draft}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no rank corresponding to 九位 (Kyū-i) was included.
== Notes ==
=== Notes === {{Reflist|group="notes"}}
=== References === {{Reflist}}
== External links ==
[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|e-Gov Law Search|ja|e-Gov法令検索|12=draft}} [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Archive <!--Senior Ninth Rank--><!--Senior Ninth Rank-->{{Court Ranks of Japan}} * <!--ranklist: {{ill|Senior First Rank|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|1=Junior First Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Senior Second Rank|ja|正二位|1=Senior Second Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank|ja|正四位|1=Senior Fourth Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位|1=Senior Sixth Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位|1=Senior Seventh Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|ja|従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Senior Eighth Rank|ja|正八位|1=Senior Eighth Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Senior Ninth Rank|ja|正九位|1=Senior Ninth Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Greater Initial Rank|ja|大初位|1=Greater Initial Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Lesser Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Lesser Initial Rank|12=draft}} endranklist--><!--Senior Ninth Rank-->

'''Shōkyū-i''' (正九位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|qq=}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>

== Overview ==

Shōkyū-i was established in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}. It was defined by the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} issued in {{ill|July 1869|ja|1869th year 7月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 1869|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and amended on {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes" name="enkaku">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref name=soutou>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref> It corresponded to positions such as officials of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] and scribes of the {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|ja|民部省 (明治時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|qq=}}.

In the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no Rank corresponding to 九位 (Kyū-i) was included.

Wikidata name: Senior Ninth Rank
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Shōkyū-i''' (正九位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|qq=}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>

== Overview ==

Shōkyū-i was established in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}. It was defined by the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} issued in {{ill|July 1869|ja|1869th year 7月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and amended on {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes" name="enkaku">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref name=soutou>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref> It corresponded to positions such as officials of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] and scribes of the {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|ja|民部省 (明治時代)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|qq=}}.

In the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no Rank corresponding to 九位 (Kyū-i) was included.

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata has short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正九位]][[ko:정9위]][[zh:正九位]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11545350]]
Wikidata name: Senior Ninth Rank
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Shōkyū-i''' (正九位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|qq=}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>

== Overview ==

Shōkyū-i was established in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}. It was defined by the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} issued in {{ill|July 1869|ja|1869th year 7月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and amended on {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes" name="enkaku">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref name=soutou>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref> It corresponded to positions such as officials of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] and scribes of the {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|ja|民部省 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|qq=}}.

In the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no Rank corresponding to 九位 (Kyū-i) was included.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正九位]][[ko:정9위]][[zh:正九位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11545350]] Wikidata name: Senior Ninth Rank [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shōkyū-i''' (正九位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|qq=}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview ==
Shōkyū-i was established in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}. It was defined by the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} issued in {{ill|July 1869|ja|1869th year 7月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and amended on {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes" name="enkaku">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref name=soutou>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref> It corresponded to positions such as officials of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] and scribes of the {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|ja|民部省 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|qq=}}.
In the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no Rank corresponding to 九位 (Kyū-i) was included.

It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|qq=}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview ==
Shōkyū-i was established in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}. It was defined by the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} issued in {{ill|July 1869|ja|1869th year 7月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and amended on {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes" name="enkaku">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref name=soutou>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref> It corresponded to positions such as officials of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] and scribes of the {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|ja|民部省 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|qq=}}.
In the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no Rank corresponding to 九位 (Kyū-i) was included.

== Overview ==
Shōkyū-i was established in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}. It was defined by the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} issued in {{ill|July 1869|ja|1869th year 7月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and amended on {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes" name="enkaku">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref name=soutou>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref> It corresponded to positions such as officials of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] and scribes of the {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|ja|民部省 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|qq=}}.
In the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no Rank corresponding to 九位 (Kyū-i) was included.

== Overview ==
Shōkyū-i was established in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}. It was defined by the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} issued in {{ill|July 1869|ja|1869th year 7月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and amended on {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes" name="enkaku">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref name=soutou>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref> It corresponded to positions such as officials of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] and scribes of the {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|ja|民部省 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|qq=}}.
In the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no Rank corresponding to 九位 (Kyū-i) was included.

== Overview ==
Shōkyū-i was established in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}} under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}. It was defined by the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} issued in {{ill|July 1869|ja|1869th year 7月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and amended on {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes" name="enkaku">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref name=soutou>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref> It corresponded to positions such as officials of the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}} and scribes of the {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|ja|民部省 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|qq=}}.
In the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no Rank corresponding to 九位 (Kyū-i) was included.

== Overview ==
Shōkyū-i was established in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}} under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}. In the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no Rank corresponding to 九位 (Kyū-i) was included.

== Overview ==
Shōkyū-i was established in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}} under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}. It was defined by the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} issued in {{ill|July 1869|ja|1869th year 7月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and amended on {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes" name="enkaku">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref name=soutou>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref> It corresponded to positions such as officials of the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}} and scribes of the {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|ja|民部省 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|qq=}}.
In the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no Rank corresponding to 九位 (Kyū-i) was included.

'''Shōkyū-i''' (正九位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|qq=}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview ==
Shōkyū-i was established in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}} under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}. It was defined by the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} issued in {{ill|July 1869|ja|1869th year 7月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and amended on {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes" name="enkaku">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref name=soutou>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref> It corresponded to positions such as officials of the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}} and scribes of the {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|ja|民部省 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|qq=}}.
In the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=Court Ranks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no Rank corresponding to 九位 (Kyū-i) was included.

Shōkyū-i was established in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}} under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}. It was defined by the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} issued in {{ill|July 1869|ja|1869th year 7月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and amended on {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes" name="enkaku">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref name=soutou>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref> It corresponded to positions such as officials of the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}} and scribes of the {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|ja|民部省 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|qq=}}.
In the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=Court Ranks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no Rank corresponding to 九位 (Kyū-i) was included.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正九位]][[ko:정9위]][[zh:正九位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正九位]][[ko:정9위]][[zh:正九位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:正九位]][[ko:정9위]][[zh:正九位]]
'''Shōkyū-i''' (正九位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} and above {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|qq=}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview ==
Shōkyū-i was established in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}} under the {{ill|Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja|太政官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōkan system (Meiji period)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太政官 (明治時代)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}}. It was defined by the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} issued in {{ill|July 1869|ja|1869th year 7月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and amended on {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes" name="enkaku">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref name=soutou>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref> It corresponded to positions such as officials of the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}} and scribes of the {{ill|Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|ja|民部省 (明治時代)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Popular Affairs (Meiji period)|qq=}}.
In the honor system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=Court Ranks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no Rank corresponding to 九位 (Kyū-i) was included.
